---
title: "Makers Workshop"
page-layout: custom
title-block-style: none
toc: false
subtitle: "Hands-on Hardware Hacking"
description: "Get hands-on with hardware hacking at the Makers Workshop on September 5, 2025 at Fab Lab in Brasov, Romania."
date: "2025-09-05"
enddate: "2025-09-05"
location: "Fab Lab, Brașov"
category: "workshop"
image: "/assets/images/makers-workshop.jpg"
registration_date: "2025-07-15"
include-in-header:
  text: |
    <style>.m { --c: #F5A623; }</style>
---

```{=html}
<div class="m">

<div class="m-hero">
  <div class="container">
    <h1>Makers <span class="gr">Workshop</span></h1>
    <p>Hands-on Hardware Hacking</p>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="m-s">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="event-dates">
      <span class="badge bg-primary">September 5, 2025</span>
      <div class="event-actions">
        <a href="/events/ical/makers-workshop-2025.ics" class="btn btn-outline-primary btn-sm" download>
          <i class="bi bi-calendar3"></i> Add to Calendar
        </a>
        <a href="/events/pdfs/makers-workshop.pdf" class="btn btn-outline-secondary btn-sm" download>
          <i class="bi bi-file-pdf"></i> Download PDF
        </a>
      </div>
    </div>
    <div class="event-location">
      <i class="bi bi-geo-alt"></i> Fab Lab, Brașov
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="m-bg">
  <div class="container">

<h2>Event Overview</h2>

<p>The Makers Workshop is a full-day, hands-on event for hardware enthusiasts, bringing together makers, DIY experts, and hardware hackers to build projects and share knowledge. Participants will have access to cutting-edge equipment, expert guidance, and a collaborative environment to turn ideas into working prototypes.</p>

<h2>Workshop Tracks</h2>

<p>Attendees can choose from multiple hands-on tracks based on their interests and experience levels:</p>

<h3>1. Microcontroller Mastery</h3>
<p>Learn to program and integrate microcontrollers like Arduino and ESP32 for IoT projects. Create smart devices that sense, connect, and respond to their environment.</p>

<h3>2. 3D Printing &amp; Modeling</h3>
<p>Master the fundamentals of 3D design and printing. Create custom parts and learn optimization techniques for functional prints.</p>

<h3>3. Electronics Fundamentals</h3>
<p>Dive into circuits, components, and soldering. Build your own electronic gadgets from basic components to functional devices.</p>

<h3>4. Advanced Robotics</h3>
<p>Design and construct simple robotic systems. Explore mechanics, control systems, and autonomous behavior.</p>

<h3>5. Wearable Technology</h3>
<p>Combine electronics with textiles and fashion. Create interactive clothing and accessories with embedded technology.</p>

<h2>Schedule</h2>

<h3>Morning Session</h3>

<table class="table">
<thead><tr><th>Time</th><th>Activity</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>08:30 - 09:00</td><td>Registration and Coffee</td></tr>
<tr><td>09:00 - 09:30</td><td>Welcome and Introduction to Tracks</td></tr>
<tr><td>09:30 - 10:30</td><td>Workshop Session 1: Fundamentals</td></tr>
<tr><td>10:30 - 10:45</td><td>Coffee Break</td></tr>
<tr><td>10:45 - 12:30</td><td>Workshop Session 2: Component Integration</td></tr>
<tr><td>12:30 - 13:30</td><td>Lunch and Maker Showcase</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h3>Afternoon Session</h3>

<table class="table">
<thead><tr><th>Time</th><th>Activity</th></tr></thead>
<tbody>
<tr><td>13:30 - 15:30</td><td>Workshop Session 3: Project Development</td></tr>
<tr><td>15:30 - 15:45</td><td>Refreshment Break</td></tr>
<tr><td>15:45 - 17:00</td><td>Workshop Session 4: Refinement and Testing</td></tr>
<tr><td>17:00 - 18:00</td><td>Project Showcase and Peer Feedback</td></tr>
<tr><td>18:00 - 19:00</td><td>Expert Panel: "The Future of Making"</td></tr>
<tr><td>19:00 - 20:00</td><td>Networking Reception and Community Building</td></tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<h2>Equipment and Resources</h2>

<p>Participants will have access to a wide range of equipment and materials:</p>

<ul>
<li>3D printers (FDM and SLA)</li>
<li>Laser cutters</li>
<li>CNC machines</li>
<li>Electronics workstations with soldering equipment</li>
<li>Microcontrollers and sensors library</li>
<li>Basic components and materials</li>
<li>Textile and soft materials workstation</li>
<li>Tools and test equipment</li>
</ul>

<h2>What You'll Take Away</h2>

<ul>
<li>A completed project in your chosen track</li>
<li>Comprehensive digital documentation of all workshop materials</li>
<li>Certificate of participation</li>
<li>New connections with the maker community</li>
<li>Knowledge of advanced fabrication techniques</li>
<li>Continuation resources for further development</li>
</ul>

<h2>Who Should Attend</h2>

<ul>
<li>Electronics hobbyists and enthusiasts</li>
<li>Students in engineering and design fields</li>
<li>Educators interested in maker education</li>
<li>Artists exploring technology integration</li>
<li>Entrepreneurs developing hardware prototypes</li>
<li>Anyone curious about making physical technology</li>
</ul>

<h2>Registration Information</h2>

<p>Registration opens on July 15, 2025. Space is limited to 75 participants to ensure quality instruction and equipment access. Early registration is recommended, as tracks fill quickly.</p>

  </div>
</div>

<div class="m-cta">
  <div class="container">
    <div class="m-d"></div>
    <h2>Turn Your Ideas Into Reality</h2>
    <p>Join us for a day of hands-on making, learning, and connecting with fellow hardware enthusiasts.</p>
    <a href="https://cal.com/chenist" target="_blank" class="m-b">Register Interest</a>
  </div>
</div>

<div class="m-bg">
  <div class="container">

<h2>Frequently Asked Questions</h2>

<h3>What skill level is required?</h3>
<p>Workshops are designed for multiple skill levels, from beginners to advanced makers. Track descriptions indicate the recommended experience level, and instructors will adapt to participant needs.</p>

<h3>Do I need to bring my own equipment?</h3>
<p>Basic materials and equipment will be provided. You're welcome to bring your laptop and any special components for your project. A detailed prep list will be sent to registered participants.</p>

<h3>Can I work on my own project instead of the track projects?</h3>
<p>Yes! While we provide structured track projects, experienced makers are welcome to work on personal projects and use the event for guidance and access to equipment.</p>

<h3>What is the registration fee?</h3>
<p>The workshop fee is &euro;120 per participant, with a reduced rate of &euro;60 for students. This covers all materials, equipment usage, meals, and refreshments during the event.</p>

<h3>Will there be take-home kits?</h3>
<p>Yes, each track includes components and materials that participants can take home to continue their projects. Additional kits will be available for purchase.</p>

  </div>
</div>

</div>
```
